# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Abstract Data Types**
+ **Structures**
+ **What's the Output?**
+ **Fill In the Missing Code**
+ **Arrays of Structures**
+ **Programming Task — Student Roster**
:::

::: notes
16.01 introduced composite types: struct groups different-typed members under one name. Exercises practiced dot notation, initialization list vs member-by-member, pass-by-reference with structs, and building a static array of structs.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- Object-Oriented Programming
- Introduction to Classes
- Creating and Using Objects
- Defining Member Functions
- Fill In the Missing Code

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_oop_paradigm.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_intro_classes.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_creating_objects.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_4_member_functions.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_6_fill_in_the_code.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- A class groups private data and public behavior under one user-defined type name
- Outside code interacts only through the public interface — the class owns its data
- Member functions defined outside the class use `ClassName::method()` syntax
- Accessors (`const`) read private data; mutators (setters) modify it and can validate before storing

---

# Next Time

::: notes
Next lecture (17.01): Arrays of Objects and Vectors of Objects — applying what students learned today about classes to collections of objects. Students will use ClassName array[n] and vector<ClassName> with the same dot-notation and method-call patterns.
:::

---

# Appendix

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: Good Practices

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: Common Mistakes

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: More Examples

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: Practice Problems

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: AI Search Terms

---

## Object-Oriented Programming: Questions for Reflection

---

## Introduction to Classes: Good Practices

---

## Introduction to Classes: Common Mistakes

---

## Introduction to Classes: More Examples

---

## Introduction to Classes: Practice Problems

---

## Introduction to Classes: AI Search Terms

---

## Introduction to Classes: Questions for Reflection

---

## Creating and Using Objects: Good Practices

---

## Creating and Using Objects: Common Mistakes

---

## Creating and Using Objects: More Examples

---

## Creating and Using Objects: Practice Problems

---

## Creating and Using Objects: AI Search Terms

---

## Creating and Using Objects: Questions for Reflection

---

## Defining Member Functions: Good Practices

---

## Defining Member Functions: Common Mistakes

---

## Defining Member Functions: More Examples

---

## Defining Member Functions: Practice Problems

---

## Defining Member Functions: AI Search Terms

---

## Defining Member Functions: Questions for Reflection

---

<!-- Arrays of Objects moved to p360_lecture_17.01 -->
